meta.title: Claude Code with LSP: Reducing Context Bloat
meta.author: Milind Nair

subtitle_lines: Claude Code can use Language Server Protocol
to navigate code surgically, reading only what matters.

cards.title: Full-file reads waste tokens
cards.body: Reading a 500-line file to understand one function buries the signal in noise.

bottom_takeaway.label: Core issue
bottom_takeaway.body: The bottleneck is how much Claude reads to find what it needs.

slides.heading: Language Server Protocol in 30 Seconds
slides.subheading: LSP is a standard protocol that lets editors and agents query a language server for structural information about code.

cards.title: A standard query interface
cards.body: LSP lets any editor or agent ask a language server structured questions: "What type is this? Where is it defined? Who calls it?"

cards.title: Compiler-grade answers
cards.body: Unlike grep, LSP understands your code structurally. It distinguishes a variable named config from the Config class.

inline_text.text: LSP gives Claude a compiler's understanding without reading every file.

bottom_takeaway.label: Key shift
bottom_takeaway.body: LSP turns "search the codebase" into "ask the compiler."

slides.heading: What Claude Code Gets from LSP

items.label: Go to Definition
items.description: Jump straight to where a symbol is declared.

items.label: Find References
items.description: See every caller without grepping the name.

bottom_takeaway.label: Net effect
bottom_takeaway.body: Targeted queries replace hundreds of lines of file reads.

slides.heading: Same Bug Fix, Different Context Cost

steps.label: Without LSP
steps.description: ~2,000 tokens consumed reading files and grepping before reasoning starts.

steps.label: With LSP
steps.description: ~200 tokens via go-to-definition and find-references. All signal.

slides.heading: Enabling LSP in Claude Code
slides.subheading: The setup overhead is close to zero for most projects.

cards.title: Automatic detection
cards.body: If your project has a language server (TypeScript, Python, Rust, Go), Claude Code detects and uses it with no configuration.

cards.title: IDE integration
cards.body: In VS Code or JetBrains, Claude Code piggybacks on the LSP server your editor already runs.

inline_text.text: If your editor has autocomplete, Claude Code can likely use LSP. Zero setup required.

bottom_takeaway.label: Prerequisite
bottom_takeaway.body: A clean build is the main requirement. A broken build means a broken language server.

slides.heading: Beyond LSP: More Ways to Cut Context
slides.subheading: LSP handles code navigation. These strategies cover the rest of the context budget.

cards.title: Use CLAUDE.md
cards.body: Give Claude upfront project context so it stops discovering things by reading files.

cards.title: Compact conversations
cards.body: Use /compact to summarize long conversations and reclaim context space.

cards.title: Write specific prompts
cards.body: "Fix the bug in auth.py:handle_login" tells Claude exactly where to look. Vague prompts force exploratory reads.

bottom_takeaway.label: Combined effect
bottom_takeaway.body: LSP is the biggest lever, but good habits multiply its effect.

slides.heading: Token Cost: Brute Force vs LSP

bottom_takeaway.label: Range
bottom_takeaway.body: LSP can cut context cost by 10-20x on navigation-heavy tasks.

slides.heading: Where LSP Falls Short
slides.subheading: LSP has clear limits. Knowing them prevents false confidence in the approach.

decisions.question: Untyped or dynamic languages?
decisions.answer: LSP provides limited info when the type system is weak or absent.

decisions.question: Config files, YAML, prose?
decisions.answer: LSP has no leverage on non-code files. Use targeted reads and CLAUDE.md.

decisions.question: Broken or missing build?
decisions.answer: LSP depends on a working language server. Build errors break queries.

decisions.question: Large monorepos with slow servers?
decisions.answer: Server latency can offset the token savings if responses take seconds.

bottom_takeaway.label: Sweet spot
bottom_takeaway.body: LSP works best in typed, buildable codebases. Outside that range, the benefits degrade quickly.

summary_lines: LSP gives Claude compiler-grade navigation without reading entire files.
10-20x token savings on common code tasks, in the right conditions.
Combine with CLAUDE.md, /compact, and specific prompts for maximum effect.
